sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Withdraw treasury funds for Hardware Wallet Maintenance 2026, covering 12 months of production maintenance for Cardano hardware-wallet support.
Recipient: Intersect 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, submitted by Intersect on behalf of the vendor.

Deliverables: Ledger and Trezor compatibility updates, Maintenance of supporting interoperability libraries and cardano-hw-cli, Developer support for ecosystem integrators, Support for integration paths involving externally maintained components where shared hardware-wallet flows intersect, Vendor-required product or security audits where firmware or app changes trigger them, Transparent capped time-and-materials reporting and milestone-based disbursement controls
Deadline/expiry: 12 months of funding for 2026; specific deadline not stated in document

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 1,310,960 ADA for Hardware Wallet Maintenance 2026, split into 1,272,777 ADA for maintenance and compatibility assurance and 38,183 ADA for the Intersect budget administration fee.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The scope is limited to compatibility, security, supporting libraries and tooling, release support, and vendor-required audit work for Cardano hardware-wallet flows, rather than building a new wallet product.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposer states that continuous maintenance is needed because Cardano protocol evolution, Ledger and Trezor release cycles, and wallet and dApp integration demand create compatibility risk for a security-critical access layer.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposer claims funding continuity in 2026 is less costly and less disruptive than waiting for breakage to accumulate and reacting after users or integrators lose access.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal says Cardano users will retain secure signing, builders will avoid broken integrations, and newer utility layers can rely on a maintained hardware-wallet path.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal states it achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase and was advanced for on-chain submission under the approved Budget Process Framework.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposer states that continuous maintenance is needed because Cardano protocol evolution, Ledger and Trezor release cycles, and wallet and dApp integration demand create compatibility risk for a security-critical access layer.

Independent evidence for: The proposer claims funding continuity in 2026 is less costly and less disruptive than waiting for breakage to accumulate and reacting after users or integrators lose access.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says Cardano users will retain secure signing, builders will avoid broken integrations, and newer utility layers can rely on a maintained hardware-wallet path.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 1,310,960 ADA for Hardware Wallet Maintenance 2026, split into 1,272,777 ADA for maintenance and compatibility assurance and 38,183 ADA for the Intersect budget administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposer states that continuous maintenance is needed because Cardano protocol evolution, Ledger and Trezor release cycles, and wallet and dApp integration demand create compatibility risk for a security-critical access layer." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
